item.title: 关于结束伊朗战争的可能协议需要了解什么
item.description:
伊斯兰堡（美联社）——美国和伊朗似乎再次接近达成协议，结束战争并开放霍尔木兹海峡。
巴基斯坦总理谢巴兹·谢里夫周六表示，旨在结束中东战争的协议比以往任何时候都更加接近，预计将在 24 小时内敲定。巴基斯坦正在准备以电子方式签署该协议，然后下周举行技术层面的会谈。
美国总统唐纳德·特朗普表示，该协议“定于明天签署”，霍尔木兹海峡将立即开放。但伊朗外交部发言人表示，周日不会签署协议，并在未来几天内保留这种可能性。
此前关于即将取得突破的声明未能实现。
本周早些时候，伊朗与美国和以色列发生交火，威胁要将该地区推入全面战争。美国和以色列于 2 月 28 日发动的战争使该地区陷入动荡，波斯湾的石油和天然气运输几乎中断。自 4 月 7 日以来，脆弱的停火已经生效。
伊朗外交部长阿巴斯·阿拉格奇周五表示，处理伊朗核计划的条款将在初步协议签署后60天内敲定，各方可以决定延长这一期限。
美国和以色列担心伊朗的核计划可能会导致核武器——这是两国领导人发动战争的主要原因。德黑兰坚称其核努力是为了和平目的。
一位根据白宫制定的基本规则要求匿名的美国政府高级官员周五表示，即将达成的协议将开始销毁或清除德黑兰高浓缩铀的进程。
这位官员表示，双方签署协议后的60天时间将用于制定移除铀的技术细节。这位官员没有透露美国打算由谁负责清除铀，据信这些铀被埋在去年遭到美国袭击的三个核设施下。
美国官员表示，即将达成的协议包括重新开放海峡的条款。
阿拉格奇表示，伊朗希望达成一项协议，允许德黑兰向通过海峡的船只“提供服务”收费。伊朗在战争期间实行了收费制度，美国和其他国家称这违反了国际法。
海峡是石油和天然气的重要运输通道，它的过境扰乱了全球能源供应，推高了燃料价格，并使食品和化肥等其他基本物资的价格远远超出该地区。
三名地区官员表示，即将达成的协议预计将包括分阶段解除对伊朗的制裁以及释放被冻结的伊朗资产。由于谈判的敏感性，这些官员要求匿名。
他们表示，预计在华盛顿和德黑兰官员批准后几天内将举行该协议的签署仪式。
伊朗坚称，任何协议还必须包括在黎巴嫩停火，以色列一直在黎巴嫩与伊朗支持的真主党激进组织作战。
以色列国防部长以色列·卡茨周五警告说，以色列仍然可以对伊朗采取独立行动，该国不会撤出在黎巴嫩、叙利亚和加沙占领的地区，也不会从以色列占领的约旦河西岸北部难民营撤军。
周六，黎巴嫩南部的战斗仍在继续。
韦瑟特从华盛顿报道，梅德尼克从以色列特拉维夫报道。美联社撰稿人米歇尔·普莱斯 (Michelle L. Price) 和阿米尔·马达尼 (Aamer Madhani) 以及佐治亚州萨凡纳的拉斯·拜纳姆 (Russ Bynum) 对本报告做出了贡献。

item.title: 特朗普和巴基斯坦表示伊朗协议可能于周日签署，但德黑兰表示需要更多时间
item.description:
伊斯兰堡（美联社）——关键调解人巴基斯坦周六表示，结束伊朗战争的协议比以往任何时候都更接近，美国也更接近达成这一协议。唐纳德·特朗普总统坚称该协议将“明天签署”，而伊朗则发表了一些迄今为止最乐观的声明，但表示还需要更多时间。
特朗普表示，霍尔木兹海峡将在签署后立即开放。
巴基斯坦总理谢巴兹·谢里夫表示，预计协议将在 24 小时内敲定。双方预计以电子方式签署。巴基斯坦外交部表示，签字仪式定于周日举行，但没有提供细节。
伊朗外交部发言人埃斯梅尔·巴加伊在官方媒体发表的声明中表示，签署“不会在明天发生”，但“在未来几天内敲定谅解备忘录的可能性很高”。
自 4 月 7 日以来，双方一直处于脆弱的停火状态。特朗普最近几周多次声称，两国即将达成协议。
伊朗长期以来对谈判表现出谨慎态度，指出去年和今年年初与美国的谈判均以美国和以色列的袭击而告终。
预计特朗普将在周一开始的七国集团峰会期间讨论霍尔木兹海峡排雷问题。
一位根据白宫规定要求匿名的美国高级官员表示，特朗普计划在七国集团峰会期间与埃及、卡塔尔和阿拉伯联合酋长国领导人会面，讨论结束战争的努力。
七国集团成员英国和法国已表示有兴趣在冲突暂停后协助排雷。
目前尚不清楚伊朗自战争开始后不久就有效控制了该海峡有多少地雷，几乎切断了波斯湾的石油和天然气运输。作为回应，美国封锁了伊朗港口。
伊朗外交部发言人表示，正在讨论的谅解备忘录的重点是结束战争，“现阶段已决定不讨论核问题”。
伊朗的核计划和高浓缩铀长期以来一直是伊朗与美国和以色列紧张关系的核心，也是国际社会关注的焦点。
特朗普在社交媒体上声称，“当一切平静时”，美国将介入并“浓缩并销毁”伊朗或美国的浓缩铀。
本周早些时候，伊朗与美国和以色列交火，威胁要打破停火协议，将中东推回全面战争之后，谈判取得了明显突破。
伊朗外交部长阿巴斯·阿拉格奇周五在 X 上发帖表示，达成协议“从未如此接近”。在威胁要夺取伊朗石油工业数小时后，特朗普周四声称谈判取得了重大进展。
伊朗国营电视台称，在战争开局袭击中丧生的前最高领袖阿亚图拉阿里哈梅内伊的葬礼将于七月举行。
哈梅内伊的葬礼、埋葬和告别活动将于 7 月 4 日至 9 日穆哈兰姆期间举行，穆哈兰姆是什叶派穆斯林日历中的传统哀悼期。
哈梅内伊的继任者是他的儿子莫吉塔巴，他被认为不那么妥协，自战争开始以来就没有公开露面。
葬礼预计将从德黑兰开始，然后移至许多什叶派高级神职人员的据点库姆，然后移至哈梅内伊的出生地马什哈德。他将被埋葬在伊玛目礼萨神社，那里被认为是什叶派信徒最神圣的地方。
1989 年阿亚图拉鲁霍拉·霍梅尼去世后，哈梅内伊重塑了伊斯兰共和国。霍梅尼是一位热情、魅力十足的理论家，领导推翻了国王，并由什叶派穆斯林神职人员建立了统治。
哈梅内伊的统治时间比霍梅尼长得多。他极大地扩大了什叶派教士阶层，并将准军事革命卫队打造为支撑其统治的最重要机构。国民警卫队成为军事和商业巨头、该国最精锐的部队和弹道导弹库的负责人，也是以色列和美国在战争中的关键目标。
马格迪从开罗报道，马达尼从华盛顿报道。美联社驻华盛顿撰稿人米歇尔·L·普莱斯 (Michelle L. Price) 撰稿。

item.title: 加拿大卡尼表示中等强国不应与美国争夺青睐
item.description:
都柏林（美联社）——加拿大总理马克·卡尼继续努力摆脱美国并与欧洲结盟，周六在即将举行的七国集团峰会之前会见了爱尔兰领导人，并表示中等强国不应与美国争夺支持。
卡尼表示，加拿大和欧盟的人口加起来是美国的两倍多，经济规模与美国相当，集体国防预算是中国的两倍。
他说，较小的国家可以通过与志同道合的盟友合作来增强自己的实力。
卡尼在都柏林三一学院表示：“在大国竞争的世界中，中等强国有一个选择：要么争夺青睐，要么联合起来创造具有影响力的第三条道路。”
他在瑞士达沃斯世界经济论坛上发表了类似的言论，当时他宣布基于规则的全球秩序已经结束，并谴责大国对小国的胁迫，该论坛成为一月份中等强国抵抗的象征。
卡尼于周六早些时候访问了爱尔兰总理迈克尔·马丁，并于周五在七国集团工​​业化民主国家峰会于法国举行之前访问了法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙。
美国总统唐纳德·特朗普周日在白宫举办 UFC 比赛庆祝他的 80 岁生日后，立即出发前往参加 G7 峰会。
一位美国政府高级官员表示，特朗普目前没有计划在峰会期间与卡尼举行双边会谈。
卡尼将加拿大和欧洲描述为“一股向善的力量——因为我们捍卫我们人民所珍视的人权、尊严和多元化价值观。”
总理表示，欧盟和加拿大是世界上最大的经济、文化、技术、金融和军事集团之一。
“新的世界秩序将从欧洲开始建立，”卡尼在早些时候与马丁举行的联合新闻发布会上表示。“加拿大是非欧洲国家中最具欧洲特色的国家。我们正在改变与欧洲的合作。”
今年二月，加拿大成为 SAFE 机制（欧盟国防采购计划）的第一个非欧洲成员。卡尼自 15 个月前就任总理以来第九次访问欧洲，他指出加拿大在关键矿产领域与 10 多个国家（主要是欧洲国家）建立了 56 个合作伙伴关系。
加拿大和美国之间的贸易紧张局势继续发酵。计划于 7 月 1 日对《美国-墨西哥-加拿大协定》(USMCA) 进行审查，该协定是北美自由贸易协定的最新版本，自 1990 年代初以来，该协定使这三个国家的经济紧密相连。特朗普本周表示，他可能不会续签该协议。
卡尼表示，加拿大贸易部长多米尼克·勒布朗将在峰会上与美国贸易代表杰米森·格里尔会面。
总理表示，他认为美国对与加拿大和墨西哥的自由贸易协定进行重大修改不感兴趣。
“美国已经很明确了。他们不想改变基本架构，”卡尼说。
卡尼强调，特朗普政府允许加拿大约85%的对美贸易免关税，因为它受到USMCA的保护。
卡尼表示，要从根本上改变协议，白宫必须向国会提交申请，并补充说白宫不想这样做。
这位不愿透露姓名的美国官员向记者介绍了白宫制定的基本规则，他表示，美国已与加拿大进行联系，以便与美国政府就贸易问题进行进一步讨论。
这位官员补充说，特朗普政府认为加拿大政府最近撤销一项监管决定，该决定要求外国流媒体平台将其加拿大收入的一部分用于资助当地新闻和节目，这是积极的决定。
但该官员补充说，预计峰会期间与加拿大“不会取得重大突破”。
特朗普本周再次表示，美国不需要加拿大拥有的任何东西。卡尼为加拿大设定了未来十年将其非美国出口增加一倍的目标，称特朗普的贸易战正在导致投资降温。
“卡尼总理非常明确和坚定地表达了加拿大加深与欧洲接触的愿望。爱尔兰热烈、毫无保留地欢迎这一雄心，我们将在即将担任主席期间尽我们所能加强欧盟与加拿大之间的关系，”马丁说。
爱尔兰将从七月开始担任欧盟理事会轮值主席国。
美联社撰稿人阿米尔·马达尼 (Aamer Madhani) 为华盛顿的这篇报道做出了贡献。

item.title: 一名女子在匹兹堡被 ICE 释放后因体温过低死亡，被裁定为他杀
item.description:
一名法医裁定一名海地寻求庇护者在被联邦拘留后死亡为他杀。她家人的代表律师表示，他希望她的亲属就她的死亡起诉移民和海关执法局。
31 岁的达菲·米歇尔 (Daphy Michel) 于 3 月 2 日去世。她被发现在匹兹堡的一个公交车候车亭。阿勒格尼县法医办公室在一份声明中表示，她的死因是体温过低，并将其判定为他杀，“表明死亡是由另一个人的行为造成的”，不应被解释为有罪宣告。该办公室周五公布了调查结果。
米歇尔是海地人，她家人的律师约瑟夫·帕特里克·墨菲 (Joseph Patrick Murphy) 表示，她于 2022 年抵达南部边境后正在美国寻求庇护。他说，由于紧急人道主义需求，她获得了人道主义假释，但她没有活着看到死后两周安排的听证会。
法医办公室表示，米歇尔在 2 月 27 日获释时是一名弱势成年人，“患有严重的心理健康问题，且没有得到治疗，并且存在严重的语言障碍”。
墨菲说，去年夏天，她因患有精神疾病而对想象中的人大喊大叫而被捕。他说，她在华盛顿县监狱关了六个月，在等待第一次听证会期间接受了多次精神检查。
墨菲说，一名地方法官表示，他不能因威胁想象中的人而对她进行审判。他说，随后，ICE 在她的牢房里逮捕了她，给她戴上脚踝监视器，然后将她带到 25 英里（40 公里）外的匹兹堡，冬天她在公共汽车候车亭坐了好几天。
墨菲说：“她穿着九月的衣服，而当时是二月，天气让她不堪重负，她体温过低。”
墨菲说，法医对他杀的认定与刑事指控不同，这意味着“有人做了或没有做某件事导致了她的死亡”。他说，他预计米歇尔的家人会就她的死亡向 ICE 提起诉讼。
美国国土安全部发言人劳伦·比斯 (Lauren Bis) 在一封电子邮件中表示，“ICE 与这名妇女的死亡无关。ICE 遇到她三天后，她就去世了。”
她称米歇尔为“来自海地的非法外国人”，米歇尔被捕后被置于遣返程序中。
比斯说，米歇尔被释放时拥有她所有的物品和一部充满电的手机，并且有公共交通。她说，米歇尔去世后的第二天，ICE 得知她的脚踝监视器“被篡改”，但县法医工作人员“拒绝与 ICE 官员合作，甚至拒绝交谈”。
比斯说，ICE 致电美国法警局，后者取回了脚踝监视器，但拒绝提供有关米歇尔状况的信息。她说，ICE 通过新闻媒体得知了她的死讯。
阿勒格尼县行政长官萨拉·伊纳莫拉托 (Sara Innamorato) 在一份声明中称米歇尔的死是“一场悲剧，如果人性一点，这件事本来是完全可以避免的。”
来自宾夕法尼亚州的民主党众议员萨默·李 (Summer Lee) 表示，她的死亡是可以预防的，“她应该得到照顾、庇护、语言支持和医疗支持。”
ICE 不再报告被拘留者在释放后 30 天内的死亡情况，结束了 2021 年拜登时代的政策。卫生专家表示，如果不解决医疗保健问题，这一变化将反映出死亡人数将少于实际发生的人数。
杜拉从北达科他州俾斯麦报道。

item.title: 纽约市庆祝尼克斯队 53 年来首个总冠军，混乱破坏了人们的喜悦
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——周六晚上，纽约尼克斯队赢得了 53 年来的首个 NBA 冠军，百老汇一片混乱，热闹的庆祝活动被混乱和暴力所破坏，其中包括时代广场的枪声。
在麦迪逊广场花园外，大屏幕上的观众欢呼雀跃，观看尼克斯队在圣安东尼奥以 16 分的落后优势击败马刺队的 NBA 总决赛第五场比赛。
不久之后，吵闹的球迷与警察发生冲突，砸碎挡风玻璃，攀爬脚手架、灯杆和雕像，爬上时代广场的校车并试图搭上一辆移动的消防车。随后，其中一辆公交车被大火吞没。
警方称，凌晨 2 点左右，第 42 街和百老汇附近发生枪击事件。旁观者的视频捕捉到至少七声枪响，显示人们蹲下并奔跑寻找掩护。警方称，初步报告显示没有人受伤。
纽约警察局表示，已有多人被捕，但要到周日晚些时候才会公布逮捕总数。
尼克斯队老板詹姆斯·多兰赛后在圣安东尼奥发表讲话，敦促球迷保持冷静。
“我们需要告诉纽约的每个人，我们知道他们正在庆祝，我们希望他们度过愉快的时光，”多兰打断了后卫乔什·哈特的新闻发布会说。“请注意安全。不要受伤，不要伤害任何人。”
该市将于周四通过游行和市政厅仪式正式庆祝尼克斯队。
周六晚上，随着终场哨声响起，主导比赛前三节的焦虑情绪被兴奋所取代。从两个月前尼克斯队首场季后赛开始的橙蓝狂热梦想最终以他们 80 年历史上的第三个总冠军而告终。
烟花在布鲁克林和中央公园上空绽放。粉丝们涌向时代广场并在街道上奔跑。在花园外，他们唱起了队歌：“加油纽约，加油纽约，加油！”
布鲁克林的警察和救护人员通过扩音器高喊“尼克斯队吧”。陌生人握手、拥抱。在林肯隧道里，人们在新泽西州大都会人寿体育场乘坐从世界杯回来的巴士，司机们鸣笛庆祝。
“我太不知所措了。我太高兴了，”来自长岛的马蒂厄·奥格诺(Mathieu Ogno)说道，在中央公园沃尔曼溜冰场举行的一场球队认可的观看派对上，他强忍着泪水，沉浸在胜利的喜悦之中。
奥格诺身穿尼克斯队队长贾伦·布伦森的球衣，他拿下45分帮助尼克斯队取得胜利，并获得了NBA总决赛MVP。布伦森坚韧不拔的决心和锐意进取的风格使他成为粉丝的最爱，体现了纽约工人阶级的精神。
尼克斯队的冠军——距上次夺冠已经过去了 19,392 天——为这支自 1999 年输给马刺队后就再也没有进入过 NBA 总决赛的球队结束了一场非凡的季后赛。自 4 月 23 日以来，球队赢得了 16 场比赛中的 15 场，唯一的一场失利发生在周一的第 3 场比赛中。
他们最后一次夺冠是在 1973 年，也是在客场第五场比赛中赢得的。他们的第一个冠军是在 1970 年，是在主场第七场比赛中惊险获胜的。两者都没有举行游行庆祝。
“我很高兴看到我的尼克斯队终于渡过难关，”26 岁的肖恩·穆内克 (Shawn Muoneke) 说道，“我见过他们敲门。过去几年他们一直在敲门。但他们终于渡过了难关，我很高兴看到这一点，也很高兴能在这座城市体验到这一点。”
穆内克出生于尼克斯队上次闯入 NBA 总决赛的一年后，他在 10 岁时就开始为尼克斯队加油。他从马里兰州开车来到这座城市，参加球队在中央公园举行的第五场比赛观看派对。
“我看到了起起落落，我看着球队重新站起来，我很高兴看到他们最终成为球队明星的最高梯队，”穆内克说。
他说，尼克斯队获胜后，这座城市的气氛“达到了前所未有的高度”。
唐纳德·特朗普总统是尼克斯队的长期球迷，他与多兰一起观看了在花园举行的第三场比赛，他在他的真相社交平台上发帖祝贺球队。
特朗普写道：“这是多么美好的一年，更重要的是，我们都见证了令人难以置信的季后赛胜利，尤其是过去的四场比赛——也许是篮球历史上最伟大的胜利。”
他补充道，随着布伦森的关键表现，“一位超级巨星诞生了。”
整个季后赛期间有数十人被捕，第三场和第四场比赛后纽约发生暴力事件，导致警察受伤，一名青少年昏迷，从周六到周日，警方做好了应对骚乱的准备。
市长佐赫兰·马姆达尼 (Zohran Mamdani) 在社交媒体上表示：“在我们庆祝的同时，要负起责任，互相照顾，保持安全，保持聪明，让这一晚能够体现我们城市最好的一面。”“我们去尼克斯吧。”
艾米丽·王·藤山 (Emily Wang Fujiyama) 贡献了报道。

item.title: 有些人正在用 3D 打印机制造枪支。一项新法律试图取消他们的印刷工作
item.description:
A first-of-its-kind law in New York could force 3D printers sold for homes and business to come equipped with technology blocking them from making guns.
The new requirement, also under consideration in California, attempts to thwart the latest technique for producing untraceable“ghost guns”that have turned up in crimes. But there are questions about whether the technology can work and concerns about its effect on personal privacy and constitutional rights.
About one-third of U.S. states already have taken steps to ban or regulate build-it-yourselffirearmsthat lack serial numbers and evade the background checks required to purchase guns from federally licensed dealers. What makes the new effort unique is that it targets the equipment used to produce the firearms, not the people who make them.
The blocking technology being pushed in two of the nation’s most populous states has the potential to set industry standards for 3D printers. It also could serve as a model for other Democratic-led states wanting to add to their gun regulations, which often already ban certainsemiautomatic weaponsand allow firearms to betemporarily confiscatedfrom people deemed to pose a threat to themselves or others.
Three-dimensional printers have become increasingly common over the past couple of decades.
Since 2012, the number of3D printersworldwide has grown from an estimated 30,000 to over 3 million while the industry’s value has multiplied from around $2 billion to $26 billion annually, said Bill Decker, executive chairman of the Association of 3D Printing. Though high-end printers cost thousands of dollars, some 3D printers now can be bought for as little as several hundred dollars.
The devices can make toys, prosthetic limbs and even airplane parts. They also can make firearms — or the pieces necessary to assemble them — using digital designs available online.Homemade gunsthat lack serial numbers often are called “ghost guns,” because they are hard for law enforcement officers to trace.
Firearms made with 3D printers are increasingly being used in crimes, according to a U.S. Department of Justice report released last year. The number of privately made guns recovered in crimes and submitted to federal authorities rose from about 1,600 in 2017 to nearly 27,500 in 2023, though the report didn’t specify how many came from 3D printers.
In a high-profile New York case, police say a 3D-printed gun likely was used tokill UnitedHealthcare’s CEOin 2024.
Ghost guns, provided by the New York City Police Department, are displayed in the Manhattan District Attorney’s office, in New York, Oct. 15, 2025. (AP Photo/Richard Drew, File)
A New York law signed last month and a bill in the California Legislature both would direct panels of experts to come up with standards for firearm blueprint detection algorithms. The technology would analyze every design submitted for 3D printing, compare it to a digital library of firearm parts, and reject those that are similar.
Though the study process would start now, the mandate that 3D printers come equipped with firearm blocking technology wouldn’t begin until 2029 — or later, in New York’s case, if the study group determines it’s not yet feasible.
The concept is a bit like a smartphone app that identifies trees or flowers from an uploaded photo, said Solomon Diamond, an associate engineering professor at Dartmouth College who was among several experts at a recent online seminar about the legislation.
For 3D printers, one possible method could use a geometric analysis of shapes, dimensions and other structural features to reject print projects that closely resemble firearm parts.
“Geometric search is mature, it’s deployed, it is ready to be applied to this problem,” said Julian Chultarsky, a technical account manager at Physna, a Columbus, Ohio-based company that develops such technology.
The Association of 3D Printing supports the legislation in New York and California, but “it’s not going to work,” Decker said. “It’s more of a political statement than anything else.”
Criminals still will come up with ways to make guns from 3D printers, either by altering their designs or taking their printing projects elsewhere, Decker said.
The more aggressive the technology becomes, the more likely that it also blocks unintended items, said Rory Mir, director of open access and technology community engagement at the Electronic Frontier Foundation, a nonprofit digital rights group. Some harmless pipes might look like gun parts, or an S-shaped wall hanger might resemble an auto sear trigger used to modify a semiautomatic weapon into a machine gun.
“These sort of censorship algorithms don’t work, and they wind up capturing and blocking a lot of lawful speech,” Mir said.
If print instructions are submitted for a cloud-based artificial intelligence search, it also risks the privacy of people’s artistic and proprietary creations, Mir said.
A handgun frame that has been printed on a 3D printer is held for a photograph at the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF), National Services Center, March 2, 2023, in Martinsburg, W.Va. (AP Photo/Alex Brandon, File)
Gun safety advocates say 3D printers have created a new pathway for people who cannot legally purchase firearms — like children or convicted felons — to nonetheless obtain them. Eleven states already generally prohibit 3D-printed guns, and six additional states require them to receive serial numbers, according to Everytown for Gun Safety.
Blocking the actual 3D printing of firearms could make it harder for people to ignore such laws.
“3D printing really is the new frontier of the fight against ghost guns,” said Samuel Levy, director of policy advocacy at Everytown for Gun Safety.
The National Rifle Association might partly agree with that assertion, though it disagrees with the policy.
“Despite desperate fear-mongering campaigns, homemade firearms are nothing new — they are a proud, time-honored American tradition dating back to the founding of our Republic,” John Commerford, executive director of the NRA Institute for Legislative Action, said in a statement. He added that “these measures only restrict responsible Americans — who do follow the law — from participating in constitutionally protected activities.”

item.title: 大胆的参议院民主党人甚至以强硬的方式阻止了两党的法案来对抗特朗普
item.description:
WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Democrats’ decision to let akey surveillance authoritylapse comes as they are increasingly emboldened in their legislative fights againstPresident Donald Trump, blocking even traditionally bipartisan bills as they push back against his policies and personnel.
The posture is an escalation from a year ago, when Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer waswidely criticizedwithin his party for a spring vote with Republicans to keep the government open. Since then, Democrats haveforced government shutdowns, slowed Trump’s nominations and nowblocked the bipartisan intelligence lawas they seek leverage in a Republican-led Congress.
The risky strategy has consequences when government programs go dark, and Democrats havelittle to show for itso far in terms of policy victories. Republicans say it is a grave threat to national security to let the surveillance law, which aims to prevent terrorist attacks, expire just as millions of people are entering the United States for World Cup games and as celebrations for the nation’s 250th anniversary get underway.
But the hardball approach has helped unite Democrats inside and outside of the Capitol as they say they have no other choice — and that the blame should fall on Trump for how he is governing.
“I don’t deny that this is dangerous,” Virginia Sen. Mark Warner, the top Democrat on the Senate Intelligence Committee, said Thursday about Democrats allowing the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act to expire starting Saturday. “But this didn’t have to happen.”
Sen. Mark Warner, D-Va., the vice chair of the Senate Intelligence Committee, speaks to reporters about FISA, the law that allows the U.S. to gather intelligence abroad, at the Capitol in Washington, Thursday, June 11, 2026. (AP Photo/J. Scott Applewhite)
Democrats’ growing confidence also comes at a time when Republicans are often sparring with Trump, who has made clear he has little interest in compromise with lawmakers in either party. Democrats are blocking renewal of the law, known as FISA, in protest of Trump’s appointment of federal housing regulator and loyalistBill Pulteto temporarily lead the nation’s intelligence agencies. The choice also rankled Republicans, who said Pulte lacks the required experience for the job.
Lawmakers in both parties urged Trump all week to pull the appointment, and on Thursday henominated a permanent replacementfor the job just after lawmakers left Washington for the weekend. But the Senate confirmation process will take time, and Trumphas not budgedon Pulte’s appointment as an interim director.
With no change, Democrats “are going to use every tool we have to fight back,” said Schumer, D-N.Y.
The standoff over FISA has won Senate Democrats some respect with base voters revolted a year ago. Schumer and the caucus have “shifted to more of a fight posture,” says Joel Payne, a Democratic strategist who served as an aide to former Senate Majority Leader Harry Reid, D-Nev.
Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D., said Democrats have been playing “fast and loose” with national security for the past year. He pointed to the 43-day government shutdown last fall and a monthslong delay in funding for Trump’s immigration enforcement operations.
“How did we get to the point where one party has completely abdicated any responsibility for our nation’s security?” Thune asked.
Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D., meets with reporters on Capitol Hill in Washington, Tuesday, June 9, 2026. (AP Photo/J. Scott Applewhite)
Democrats argue that Pulte, with little national security background, is a greater threat. They note that as a federal housing regulator, he has pushed for investigations of high-profile political figures whom Trump considers political foes.
“It’s not a close call,” Sen. Chris Murphy, D-Conn. “We cannot extend these capabilities if the president is making clear that he’s going to use them not to protect the nation, but to protect himself politically.”
Strategist Payne says he believes Democrats have gained a bit of leverage since the shutdown in the fall.
Democrats did not get the extension in health care subsidies they demanded because a small group of moderate Democratsvoted with Republicans to end the impasse. They did not achieve the changes to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol that they sought when they delayed passage of funding for those agencies for months. But the White House didagree to negotiate, even if those talks ultimately failed.
Democrats also have grown more unified. While moderates ended the fall shutdown, the party stayed together in blocking the immigration funding and the surveillance authority.
“They’ve showed Republicans they are not going to fold,” Payne said.
Still, it may not be enough for some in the party base or to win Democrats a majority in November’s midterm elections.
Andrew O’Neill, national advocacy director for the Democratic resistance group Indivisible, said he was concerned to see some Democrats praise Jay Clayton, Trump’s permanent pick for the intelligence job.
Jay Clayton, U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York leaves a news conference in New York, March 9, 2026. (AP Photo/Seth Wenig, File)
Republicans are rushing to confirm Clayton, the U.S. attorney for the Southern District of New York and a former Securities and Exchange Commission chairman, before Tulsi Gabbard leaves the job and Pulte takes over as interim director on June 19.
It is unclear, so far, if Democrats will support Clayton or allow Republicans to speed up the process and confirm him quickly.
O’Neill said he is glad Democrats blocked FISA over Pulte’s appointment, but activists are wary.
“It’s a mixed bag,” O’Neill said of the past year. “The frustration is it took so long.”
President Donald Trump speaks during an event to sign a proclamation about the fishing industry, in the Oval Office of the White House, Thursday, June 11, 2026, in Washington. (AP Photo/Jacquelyn Martin)
Caught in the middle are Senate Republicans, who had to spend months to fund border enforcement agencies and are now navigating the dispute over FISA, even after lawmakers reached a bipartisan compromise.
Republicans are also trying to work with Trump, who derailed the intelligence legislation when he announced Pulte’s appointment as senators were on the verge of passing that deal.
Trump has weakened Republicans’ position — and his own support in the Senate — by backing primary challenges to incumbent senators. Republican Sens.John Cornyn of TexasandBill Cassidy of Louisianaboth lost in primaries to Trump-backed opponents last month and have joined Democrats in criticizing Pulte.
Senate Democrats say hope their strategy gives them enough leverage to win more Republican allies.
Being in the minority is “a difficult dynamic for us,” said Sen. Peter Welch, D-Vt. “But I’m seeing the Republicans start to move a little bit.”

item.title: 法官命令特朗普政府恢复国家公园对“贬低”美国的地点的改变
item.description:
周五，一名联邦法官下令特朗普政府恢复根据一项行政命令更改的地点，该命令要求国家的博物馆、公园和地标建筑不得展示“不适当地贬低美国人过去或现在”的元素。
美国马萨诸塞州地区法官安吉尔·凯利发布的初步禁令还下令暂停任何额外的改变，并写道，原告已经表明这些努力的目的是“用涂白的笔改写国家的历史”。
法官写道：“在忠实讲述历史的同时，不能排除社区的经历，这些社区的贡献、斗争和成就构成了我们国家故事的重要组成部分。”
法官写道，特朗普政府还必须每周提供一份状态报告，描述他们在这些变革方面取得的进展。
凯利写道：“本届政府打着提升美国尊严的幌子，试图通过下令拆除国家公园内所有不符合其偏好叙述的标志、展示和解说性展览来分享有限的历史，从而讲述了半真半假的故事。”
该命令是对保护和历史组织今年二月就国家公园管理局政策提起的诉讼做出的回应，这些组织称这些政策迫使公园管理局工作人员删除或审查数十件分享真实准确且相关的美国历史和科学知识（包括奴隶制和气候变化）的展品。
其中许多变化发生在费城的独立国家历史公园，政府拆除了有关 1790 年代美国第一任总统乔治·华盛顿 (George Washington) 统治下的 9 名奴隶生活的展品。其他变化包括移除亚利桑那州日落火山口火山国家纪念碑上描述玄武岩气泡的标志，因为上面有一个游客举着骄傲旗帜的图像，而有关劳工历史的电影则从马萨诸塞州洛厄尔国家历史公园移除。
去年，唐纳德·特朗普总统在国家博物馆、公园和地标建筑上签署了“恢复美国历史的真相和理智”的行政命令。内政部长道格·布尔古姆莱特下令从联邦政府控制下的博物馆、纪念碑、地标建筑和其他公共展览中清除“不当党派意识形态”。
周六发送了一封寻求内政部置评的电子邮件。
提起诉讼的组织之一国家公园保护协会文化资源高级主任艾伦·斯皮尔斯表示，这一裁决将有助于保护国家公园免受政府“抹去这些独一无二的地方的历史和科学”的影响。
“国家公园属于美国人民，任何形式的审查制度都违背了这些地方所代表的价值观，”他说。
另一个提起诉讼的组织国家公园巡游者协会的执行董事比尔·韦德表示，这对于“为能够提供真实、准确和公正的信息而自豪”的国家公园员工来说尤其是个好消息。

item.title: 特朗普在白宫举行的不同寻常的 UFC 格斗之夜吸引了人群
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——这些身材魁梧的综合格斗选手一一走过庄严、庞大的美国第十六任总统大理石雕像，慢跑下林肯纪念堂的台阶，成千上万的球迷欢呼雀跃，参加了这个纪念美国建国 250 周年和唐纳德·特朗普总统 80 岁生日的不同寻常的体育周末。
周五晚上的新闻发布会上，准备周日在白宫外八角大楼内对决的战士们亮相。但这也是一个见到 UFC 粉丝的机会，他们涌入华盛顿，忍受闪电、潮湿和虫子的折磨。
2026 年 6 月 12 日星期五，来自北卡罗来纳州夏洛特的特雷西·菲尔贝克 (Tracy Philbeck) 和他的儿子利维·菲尔贝克 (Levi Philbeck) 在华盛顿林肯纪念堂合影留念。（美联社照片/法蒂玛·侯赛因）
特雷西·菲尔贝克 (Tracy Philbeck) 和他的儿子利维 (Levi) 与一群朋友从北卡罗来纳州夏洛特开车过来，支持他们最喜欢的拳手美国贾斯汀·盖瑟 (Justin Gaethje)，参加即将到来的轻量级冠军赛，对手是格鲁吉亚人伊利亚·托普里亚 (Ilia Topuria)。
“当贾斯汀·盖瑟获胜时，你会听到老鹰的尖叫声，”老菲尔贝克笑着说。
大卫·霍尔斯特德 (David Halstead) 从西澳大利亚州奥尔巴尼 (Albany) 出发，观看这项他热爱了十年的运动。霍尔斯特德表示，经常参加比赛的特朗普“让 UFC 名声大噪”。
2026 年 6 月 12 日星期五，来自西澳大利亚州奥尔巴尼的大卫·霍尔斯特德 (David Holstead) 在华盛顿林肯纪念堂摆姿势拍照。（美联社照片/法蒂玛·侯赛因）
UFC 表示在本周末的庆祝活动上花费了 6000 万美元，共和党总统称其为“地球上最伟大的表演”。
公共诚信项目在监管机构提起的诉讼中将这一事件描述为“私人、商业、腐败地利用我们最神圣的国家纪念碑谋取私利”，试图阻止这一事件在联邦土地上发生。一名联邦法官周五裁定，白宫获准继续审理此案。
根据益普索体育 (Ipsos Sports) 在二月和三月进行的民意调查，只有大约十分之一的美国成年人认为自己是综合格斗爱好者。该民意调查显示综合格斗粉丝以男性和非白人为主。他们更有可能认为自己是共和党人而不是民主党人。
“一个误解是，所有观看 UFC 比赛的人都是特朗普的支持者，但事实并非如此，”24 岁的里卡多·罗德里格斯 (Ricardo Rodriguez) 说道，他解释说，他热爱这项运动的身体对抗性。“人们每次都期待着被淘汰，”他说。
2026 年 6 月 12 日星期五，来自佛罗里达州代托纳比奇的雅各布·伯维斯 (Jacob Burvis) 和埃莉·路易斯 (Ellie Louises) 在华盛顿林肯纪念堂摆姿势拍照。（美联社照片/法蒂玛·侯赛因）
艾莉·路易斯 (Ellie Louises) 练习泰拳和柔术，她和男友雅各布·珀维斯 (Jacob Purvis) 一起从佛罗里达州代托纳海滩开车过来。
MMA 的女性粉丝是少数。但路易斯说，她认识很多通过男性伴侣观看这项运动的女性。她说“女性战士通常比男性战士更具攻击性”。
林肯纪念堂的球迷对在白宫举行的比赛的批评不予理睬。
特雷西·菲尔贝克说，在“人民之家”举行斗殴活动“可以追溯到泰迪·罗斯福时代。”
罗斯福定期在白宫举行拳击比赛，尽管它们不是正式的公开拳击比赛。他是一位热情的业余拳击手，曾在哈佛打过拳击，并在一生中的大部分时间里继续这项运动。
拳击迷也占 UFC 粉丝群的很大一部分。
中城青年学院执行董事格洛丽亚·李 (Gloria Lee) 于 2026 年 6 月 10 日星期三在华盛顿举行的一场活动中与 UFC 拳手兰迪·布朗 (Randy Brown) 进行对决。（美联社照片/法蒂玛·侯赛因）
本周，在哥伦比亚特区中城青年学院举办的由 UFC 赞助的社区活动中，拳击馆的执行董事为 UFC 拳手兰迪·布朗的到访提供帮助，后者与当地十几名青少年和青春期前的青少年进行了拳击比赛。
格洛丽亚·李 (Gloria Lee) 表示，对于她健身房里的孩子们来说，见到这位拳手是件大事。“这真是令人兴奋的一周，当他进门时我正要摔倒！”她说。
当被问及她个人的 UFC 粉丝群体时，李说她没有看过太多。但在布朗访问结束时，她和职业拳击手一起进入了拳击场，并扔出了一些她自己的子弹。
美联社撰稿人林利·桑德斯为本报告做出了贡献。

item.title: 复出的尼克斯队是冠军尼克斯队。布伦森拿下 45 分，纽约队击败马刺队夺冠
item.description:
SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Jalen Brunson and the Comeback Knicks did it again. And now they’re the Champion Knicks.
For the first time in 53 years, New York rules the NBA. Brunson scored 45 points, including 13 straight for New York in the fourth quarter, and the Knicks beat the San Antonio Spurs 94-90 in Game 5 of the NBA Finals on Saturday night.
The Knicks won the series 4-1, rallying from double-digit deficits in all four of those victories. The deficit was 16 on Saturday night. Brunson and the Knicks were never fazed.
“I have no words,” Brunson, the NBA Finals MVP, said during the on-court celebration. “It’s everything I ever dreamed of.”
Brunson, fittingly, closed with a flourish. He set a Knicks record for points in a finals game; it had been 38 by Willis Reed against the Los Angeles Lakers in Game 3 of the 1970 series. It now belongs to the left-handed point guard who changed the franchise’s fortunes when he arrived four years ago.
“It’s surreal,” Knicks coach Mike Brown, who was hired a year ago — making him the franchise’s 24th coach since the franchise’s last championship in 1973. “I still can’t believe it’s happened.”
Mikal Bridges and Josh Hart — the other two parts of the “Nova Knicks” trio that also includes Brunson, three players who were NCAA champions at Villanova and teamed up in New York to try to do the same — combined to score 27 points. Bridges had 14, Hart 13.
“I don’t know what I’m feeling,” Brunson said. “I’m in awe. Whenever someone counted us out, we found a way to come back and do something about it.”
Dylan Harper scored 25 for the Spurs, who got 19 points, 14 rebounds and five blocked shots from Victor Wembanyama.
“This is the biggest lesson of my life, the biggest learning moment,” Wembanyama said. “I can’t tell exactly what the lesson is, but we’re learning.”
The Knicks improved to 4-0 in closeout opportunities this season, winning them all on the road. It didn’t feel like the road, though — not with thousands of New York faithful having made the trip to Texas to see a moment 53 years in the making.
The Knicks rallied from a 16-point deficit to beat the Spurs in San Antonio in Game 5 of the NBA Finals, turning pensive crowds euphoric at watch parties outside the team’s Madison Square Garden arena and in parks and streets across the city.
And back home, on the streets of the Big Apple, celebrations broke out everywhere. Fireworks lit up the night sky, people honked horns on jampacked streets and firefighters — from their trucks — slapped high-fives with delirious fans.
“HISTORY,” New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani wrote on social media, then added that the Knicks’ championship parade will be Thursday.
New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani celebrated the Knicks win in a bar on Manhattan’s Canal Street on Saturday night. For the first time in 53 years, New York rules the NBA, with Jalen Brunson scoring 45 points, including 13 straight for New York in the fourth quarter.
New York got to the brink of this title by rallying from 29 points down inGame 4to win 107-106 onOG Anunoby’s tip-inwith 1.2 seconds left on Wednesday night. It was the largest comeback in NBA Finals history and the biggest comeback in any game this season, regular season or playoffs.
By comparison, then, a 16-point rally in this one seemed easy. And San Antonio had to shuffle off into the offseason, listening to Knicks fans celebrating in their building.
“We weren’t ready to win an NBA championship,” Spurs coach Mitch Johnson said. “The better team won. We did a lot of good things, and we didn’t finish the job. That’s what it is.”
The game followed the same script in the opening minutes as all the others in the series, with the Spurs taking a double-digit lead in the first quarter and then frittering most of it away in the second quarter.
The Spurs became the first team in the play-by-play era, which started in the 1996-97 season, to lead five finals games by 10 points or more in first quarters.
The Knicks simply could not make a shot, missing on 16 of their first 18 tries and each of their first 11 two-point attempts. There even was a point in the second quarter when Wembanyama had more blocked shots (five) than the Knicks had made shots (four). San Antonio’s lead was as many as 10 in the first quarter, as many as 16 in the second.
Of course, none of it mattered much. As always, the Knicks came back.
A 22-9 run in the second quarter got New York within three, before Devin Vassell scored just before the halftime buzzer to give San Antonio a 42-37 edge at the break.
And that capped an opening 24 minutes of either offensive ineptitude or defensive prowess, depending on perspective. The 79 combined points in the first half were the lowest in a finals game since Game 7 of Lakers-Celtics in 2010, and the combined 31.8% field goals shooting by the Knicks and Spurs was the lowest in the first half of a finals game in the play-by-play era.
Brunson won NCAA crowns twice with Villanova — both in Texas, the 2016 one in Houston and the 2018 one in San Antonio, just a few miles away from the arena that the Spurs call home.
A Texas three-step of titles, and this one was surely the sweetest of all.
“It’s why I came to New York,” Brunson said.

item.title: 湾区举办世界杯，李维斯体育场到处都是空荡荡的红色座位
item.description:
加利福尼亚州圣克拉拉（美联社）——周六世界杯上，卡塔尔令人惊讶地 1-1 战平瑞士，李维斯体育场内散布着数千个空座位。
按照湾区的标准，六月的一个异常温暖的下午，开球时的气温约为 82 华氏度（28 摄氏度），许多空座位都位于体育场的东侧，通常在一年中的大部分时间里都会变得非常炎热。
瑞士球迷将球场涂成鲜红色，与空荡荡的红色座位融为一体。
官方公布的观赛人数为 67,966 人，接近世界杯比赛的官方容量 68,827 人。
周五，国际足联将韩国与捷克在瓜达拉哈拉举行的世界杯比赛期间的空座位归咎于在大厅观看比赛的球迷。
在李维斯体育场 (Levi’s Stadium) 观看 NFL 比赛时，观众经常会因为在硅谷中心的场馆外尾随而迟到，但世界杯比赛不允许这样做。这使得周六的入场得以顺利进行，因为在开球前几个小时，场外就排起了小队。
李维斯体育场可容纳 68,500 人观看 NFL 比赛，但可以扩展到 70,000 多人观看足球比赛。两年前，巴西和哥伦比亚在美洲杯小组赛中吸引了 70,971 人。
仅四个月前，圣克拉拉体育场就举办了超级碗比赛。
美联社世界杯：https://apnews.com/fifa-world-cup

item.title: 詹姆斯·哈登因在机动车辆中携带武器的轻罪指控在休斯顿被捕
item.description:
据哈里斯县地方书记官办公室称，克利夫兰骑士队球员詹姆斯·哈登于周六在休斯敦被捕，罪名是在机动车辆中非法携带武器。
指控文件显示，哈登于凌晨 3 点 41 分被捕，并于凌晨 5 点前被登记，随后交纳了 100 美元的保释金，并被警方释放。指控文件显示，哈登有一把手枪，但不在枪套里，看得很清楚。
据书记员办公室网站称，他的提审定于 6 月 22 日进行。法庭文件中将哈登列为休斯顿居民。
克利夫兰骑士队在一份声明中表示：“克利夫兰骑士队今天早上知道詹姆斯·哈登被捕，并且正在收集更多信息。”“我们正在与詹姆斯和他的代表联系，并将继续关注事态的发展。目前，我们不会发表进一步的评论。”
美联社向哈登代表发送寻求评论的消息没有立即得到回复。
现年36岁的哈登刚刚结束了自己的第17个NBA赛季，有权行使4230万美元的选择权在下赛季重返骑士队。当克利夫兰的季后赛之旅在东部决赛输给纽约尼克斯队时，他表示他希望留在俱乐部，这表明他愿意放弃选择，转而与骑士队签订更长的合同。
哈登 11 次入选全明星，8 次入选 NBA 最佳阵容，并在 2017-18 赛季为休斯敦火箭队效力期间获得 MVP 荣誉。
美联社篮球作家蒂姆·雷诺兹为本报告做出了贡献。

item.title: 你可以忽略像 SpaceX 这样的人工智能巨头，但你的 401(k) 不会
item.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — While you might want to ignore all the hubbub aroundSpaceX, Elon Musk and IPOs, your 401(k) likely can’t.
SpaceX is now worth $2.1 trillion after its stock launched 19.2% higher in its debut on Wall Street. Whether or not you believe it deserves to be worth more than Exxon Mobil, Bank of America and Coca-Cola combined, the collective market does. And if SpaceX maintains that big a value, it will join some high-profile stock indexes.
Many of these indexes don’t care about how realistic a company’s growth plans are or who its CEO is. They’re simply trying to show how slices of the market, or the whole thing, are performing. And if SpaceX is big enough to meet the qualifications to join those indexes, whether it’s in a few weeks or a year, it will gain entry.
That matters for investors and their 401(k) accounts because they’re depending more than ever on funds that simply mimic these indexes. It’s a lower-cost way to invest, allowing savers to keep more of their investments. Partly because of that, such index funds have usually proven to be better performers than funds that try to pick and choose individual stocks.
Just one in five actively managed U.S. stock funds survived and beat their average index peer over the last decade, at 21%, according to Morningstar’s data through 2025. Such disparities in performance meant investors had more money invested in U.S. index funds than actively managed ones beginning in 2024, and the gap has only grown since then.
They’re things the investment industry has created to answer the question: What is the market doing? It’s otherwise tough to answer quickly when the U.S. market has thousands of stocks moving in different directions at any moment.
The S&P 500 is perhaps the most famous and influential index. It tracks 500 of the biggest U.S. stocks, and trillions of dollars in investments are either directly mimicking it or at least benchmarking themselves against it.
The Dow Jones Industrial Average is well known because it’s been around since the 19th century, but it tracks only 30 big stocks so Wall Street pays it little attention.
Because index funds are the way so many investors put money into the stock market, companies want to be part of indexes. Stocks can see a big jump in their prices after S&P Dow Jones Indices, Nasdaq, FTSE Russell or other companies announce they’ll be joining their indexes.
The investment industry has created funds, including both traditional mutual funds and exchange-traded funds, to track almost every kind of index. More than 1,000 index funds were available at the end of last year, according to the Investment Company Institute. Of them, 185 tracked the S&P 500.
Nasdaq changed its rules to allow some huge companies to join its Nasdaq 100 index after just 15 trading days. That’s a break from the past, where it would wait until each December to add new members in an annual reconstitution to make sure it includes the 100 largest non-financial companies on the Nasdaq.
Some popular funds track the Nasdaq 100 index, including the QQQ exchange-traded fund from Invesco that has roughly $477 billion in total investments. That means QQQ holders could soon own shares of SpaceX, without doing anything on their own.
Anthropic and OpenAI are two other huge AI-related companies looking to sell their own stocks soon on a U.S. exchange for the first time. Their IPOs could potentially make each worth close to $1 trillion.
It used to be that companies would have an IPO long before they got that big. But SpaceX, Anthropic and OpenAI swelled to tremendous sizes thanks to dollars from private investors, including pension funds, companies and rich investors, away from the public market.
That’s forcing the reconsideration for the investment industry about how quickly to add companies to indexes that they say track the biggest companies.
The company behind the S&P 500 isnot making changesto allow SpaceX and other “mega” IPOs faster entry into the index. For it, a stock needs to trade on an eligible exchange for at least 12 months before it can join the index.
Not only that, S&P Dow Jones Indices also requires companies to have made a profit in its most recent quarter and over the sum of its last four quarters.
SpaceXlost $4.9 billion last yearand another $4.3 billion through the first three months of 2026. It acknowledges that it “may not achieve profitability in the future.” Over the long term, a stock’s price tends to track with how much profit the company is making.
Officials from pension funds for firefighters, teachers and other workers in California and New York sent a letter to SpaceX last month decrying its corporate governance, including how much power Musk will hold over the company through his ownership of a special class of stock with more voting power.
They said they could become owners of SpaceX stock because they hold index funds.
If Musk is able to control so much of the voting power on the board of directors, it would make him tremendously powerful atop SpaceX, “essentially making him unfireable without his own consent,” the CEO of California Public Employees’ Retirement System, the New York state comptroller and the New York City comptroller wrote in their letter.
Index funds track indexes. And if a stock is in an index, the index fund will buy it, even if investors may not like it.
Tesla has remained in the S&P 500 even though critics called it overvalued for years, for example, and Musk’s electric-vehicle company has grown to become one of Wall Street’s 10 biggest companies.
Some indexes say they will not include companies that have poor corporate governance standards or other narrowed criteria, but investors need to look for them.
The S&P 500 ESG index famouslykicked Tesla out in 2022, for example.

item.title: 从火箭到大脑植入物，让我们来看看埃隆·马斯克的庞大帝国
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——埃隆·马斯克是世界首富，也是有史以来第一位万亿富翁，控制着许多不同的企业。
电动汽车。大脑植入物。地下隧道。一个曾经被称为 Twitter 的社交媒体平台。还有一家火箭制造商本周在华尔街进行了交易。
随着时间的推移，越来越多的企业发现自己处于同一屋檐下。今年早些时候，马斯克将周五上市的 SpaceX 与其人工智能公司 xAI 合并。但如今，除了其他各种高管头衔或所有权股份外，他仍然在多家公司担任首席执行官一职。
让我们来看看马斯克庞大的商业帝国。
马斯克于 2002 年创立了 SpaceX，担任首席执行官。该公司的发展已远远超出了火箭领域。它拥有卫星通信服务 Starlink，这是该公司的一大现金来源，去年营业收入达 44 亿美元。SpaceX 还拥有社交媒体平台 X（以前称为 Twitter），马斯克于 2022 年以 440 亿美元的价格收购了该平台，并将其归入了 Grok 聊天机器人的制造商 xAI 旗下。
xAI 和 X 都是亏损者（去年 AI 业务亏损了 64 亿美元）。尽管如此，去年运营总亏损 26 亿美元的 SpaceX 仍能引起足够的市场炒作，在周五进行了历史上规模最大的首次公开募股，收盘价略低于每股 161 美元，总市值达到 2.1 万亿美元。
一些人认为这个价格明显高估了该公司的价值。SpaceX 承诺将成为人工智能领域的领导者，并有一天帮助人类在多行星上生活——其崇高的、有时听起来很科幻的目标包括从将数据中心置于太空到殖民火星等。但这很大程度上取决于未经证实的技术和大量的资本需求。
马斯克还是特斯拉的首席执行官，自 2008 年以来他一直在这家电动汽车制造商担任首席执行官。
特斯拉一直在努力应对电动汽车领域日益激烈的竞争。去年，该公司将全球最大电动汽车制造商的桂冠让给了中国比亚迪。由于马斯克的政治抵制，销量也受到了打击。此后，这些数字有所回升，但马斯克一再摆脱困境——强调特斯拉的未来不在于汽车销售，而在于让人们乘坐自动驾驶出租车。
除了道路之外，特斯拉还一直在提高家庭和企业机器人的产量。通过收购由马斯克和他的两个堂兄弟创立的 SolarCity，该公司涉足太阳能业务已有约十年的时间。特斯拉于 2010 年上市，随后加入了标准普尔 500 指数的万亿美元俱乐部。目前其市值约为 1.5 万亿美元。
马斯克还在 2016 年与他人共同创立的脑机接口公司 Neuralink 担任首席执行官。
Neuralink 是致力于将人类神经系统与机器连接起来的众多团体之一。它针对患有脊髓损伤、ALS 和其他疾病的人启动了临床试验。该公司（有时还有马斯克本人）近年来宣布了一些大脑植入产品。一月份，Neuralink 表示其在全球范围内有 21 名试验参与者。
马斯克还创立了 The Boring Company，这是一家拥有十年历史的隧道挖掘和地下运输公司。
Boring Company 是“维加斯环路”等项目的幕后黑手，这是一个地下特斯拉网约车隧道网络，于 2021 年在拉斯维加斯会议中心周围首次开通。该公司承诺提供高速交通网络，还计划在迪拜和纳什维尔建造隧道。尽管如此，一路上阻力还是不断增加。该公司被指控违反了拉斯维加斯的多项安全和环境要求，其全线尚未完工，并且还受到纳什维尔一些当地官员的批评。
马斯克通过创建两家公司 Zip2 和 PayPal（曾经的 X.com）获得了最初的财富。这些当时的初创公司在几十年前就被卖给了新的所有者，但他在出售时获得了约 2 亿美元，马斯克后来用这笔钱创办了 SpaceX 并投资了特斯拉。

item.title: OpenAI 因 IPO 临近而受到多州调查可能造成的用户伤害
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——OpenAI 收到了来自多个州的传票，作为对其聊天机器人用户安全性调查的一部分，该公司正准备首次向公众发行股票。
广受欢迎的聊天机器人 ChatGPT 背后的公司表示，它将“建设性地”回应调查，并且已经采取了保护客户的措施。
一位发言人在一份电子邮件声明中表示：“人工智能是一项强大的新技术，我们每天都在努力以负责任的方式安全地为人们带来好处。”“我们认真对待州总检察长提出的担忧。”
OpenAI 因 ChatGPT 涉嫌向想要自杀或参与犯罪行为的用户提供鼓励言论而受到批评。它还因其如何使用客户的健康数据和其他个人信息而受到审查。
周四，该公司被一名加拿大人起诉，指责聊天机器人导致了她女儿上吊自杀的决定。6 月初，佛罗里达州总检察长在发生两起枪击事件后对该公司提起诉讼，据报道，据称枪手在计划犯罪时向 ChatGPT 提出了问题。
OpenAI 在一份声明中表示，其模型反复鼓励人们寻求现实世界的支持，包括心理健康专业人士的支持。该公司还表示，在两起枪击案件中都与执法部门合作。
新的调查是在该公司向美国安全监管机构提交备受期待的首次公开募股文件的几天后进行的。人工智能竞争对手 SpaceX 于周五庆祝了自己的 IPO。这家由埃隆·马斯克创立的火箭制造商还经营一家人工智能业务，负责开发名为 Grok 的竞争对手聊​​天机器人。
政府应如何应对人工智能的潜在好处和可能的危险正在成为一个重大的政治问题。
欧洲监管机构对马斯克的 Grok 展开调查，调查内容涉及反犹太主义内容和色情内容，其中包括深度伪造裸体内容。另一家准备首次公开募股的聊天机器人公司 Anthropic 周五接到特朗普政府的指示，出于国家安全原因，关闭了其向海外用户提供的两个在线模型。
《华尔街日报》早些时候报道了 OpenAI 传票。
美联社周六向十几个州总检察长发送了电子邮件，询问调查的细节，但尚未收到任何回复。
OpenAI 在声明中强调了其为确保儿童使用其聊天机器人的安全而采取的措施。
声明中写道：“今天的 ChatGPT 为未成年人和遇到困难的人提供了更具保护性的体验，并提供了引导他们接触现实世界资源和值得信赖的人际交往的保障措施。”“我们相信孩子应该像孩子一样对待，这就是为什么我们建立了年龄预测，发布了家长工具来指导孩子使用人工智能，并禁止针对儿童的广告。”
